solution: Lewis acids can initiate the cationic polymerization.

subject: Chemistry
q: Synthetic polymer bakelite can be prepared from following compounds
question: Synthetic polymer bakelite can be prepared from following compounds
solution: Phenol and formaldehyde undergo condensation polymerisation under two different conditions to give a cross linked polymer called bakelite.

subject: Chemistry
q:
Match List I with List II

  List-I (Polymer)   List-II (Nature)
A

I Thermosetting polymer
B

II Fibers
C

III Elastomer
D

IV Thermoplastic polymer

Choose the correct answer from the options given below
question:
Match List I with List II

  List-I (Polymer)   List-II (Nature)
A

I Thermosetting polymer
B

II Fibers
C

III Elastomer
D

IV Thermoplastic polymer

Choose the correct answer from the options given below
solution:
The Thermosetting Polymers tend to have a cross-linked 3D structure.

Bakelite or Novolac are examples of Thermosetting Polymer.

Polymer fibers are a subset of man-made fibers, which are based on synthetic chemicals (often from petrochemical sources) rather than arising from natural materials by a purely physical process. These fibers are made from: polyamide nylon.

Thermoplastics are a class of polymers that can be softened and melted by the application of heat, and can be processed either in the heat-softened state (e.g. by thermoforming) or in the liquid state (e.g. by extrusion and injection molding). polyvinyl chloride is an example of it.

Elastomer, any rubbery material composed of long chainlike molecules, or polymers, that are capable of recovering their original shape after being stretched to great extents - hence the name elastomer, from "elastic polymer."

Neoprene is elastomer

Nylon-6,6 is fiber

PVC is thermoplastic

Novolac is thermosetting

solution:
Ziegler-Natta is used in the production of high-density polymer.
The addition polymerisation of Acrylonitrite in presence of a peroxide catalyst leads to the formation of Polyacrylonitrile
Nylon - 6 is a condensation polymer made from caprolactum
Novolac is the linear product of phenol-formaldehyde which is produced by condensation reactions in the presence of either acid or base.

subject: Chemistry
q:
Match List I with List II

  List I (Name of polymer)   List II (Uses)
A Glyptal I Flexible pipes
B Neoprene II Synthetic wool
C Acrilan III Paints and Lacquers
D LDP IV Gaskets

Choose the correct answer from the options given below :-
question:
Match List I with List II

  List I (Name of polymer)   List II (Uses)
A Glyptal I Flexible pipes
B Neoprene II Synthetic wool
C Acrilan III Paints and Lacquers
D LDP IV Gaskets

Choose the correct answer from the options given below :-
solution:
(A) Glyptal polymer is used as a manufacture of paints and Lacquers

(B) Neoprene is formed by the free radical polymerisation of chloroprene. It is used for manufacturing conveyor belts, gaskets and hoses.

(C) Polyacrylonitrile is used as a substitute for wool in making commercial fibres as orlon or acrilan.

(D) Low density polythene (LDP) is chemically inert and tough but flexible and a poor conductor of electricity. Hence, it is used in the insulation of electricity carrying wires and manufacture of squeeze bottles, toys and flexible pipes.

solution:
Neoprene (CR), also called polychloroprene or chloroprene rubber, synthetic rubber produced by the polymerization (or linkage together of single molecules into giant, multiple-unit molecules) of chloroprene.

Natural rubber is polyisoprene. It is a polymer of hydrocarbon-isoprene. Polyisoprene is an addition polymer. It is cis-1, 4-polyisoprene.

Teflon is a brand name for a synthetic chemical called polytetrafluoroethylene (PTFE). Orlon is made from polymerized acrylonitrile.

options: Both <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">R</mi></math> are correct and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>R</mi></math> is the correct explanation of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi></math>

<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></math> is correct but <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">R</mi></math> is not correct.

<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></math> is not correct but <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">R</mi></math> is correct.

subject: Chemistry
q:
Assertion: Rayon is a semisynthetic polymer whose properties are better than natural cotton.

Reason: Mechanical and aesthetic properties of cellulose can be improved by acetylation.
question:
Assertion: Rayon is a semisynthetic polymer whose properties are better than natural cotton.

Reason: Mechanical and aesthetic properties of cellulose can be improved by acetylation.
options: Both assertion and reason are correct, but the reason is not the correct explanation for the assertion.
Both assertion and reason are correct, and the reason is the correct explanation for the assertion.
Assertion in incorrect statement, but the reason is correct.
Both assertion and reason are incorrect.
solution: The strength of cellulose is improved by acetylation and then used for making packing material. Rayon (semisyntehtic polymer of cellulose) has superior properties than natural cotton.

solution:
Polyvinyl chloride (PVC) : Manufacture of rain coats, hand bags, vinyl flooring, water pipes.

Polystyrene : As insulator, wrapping material, manufacture of toys, radio and television cabinets.

Bakelite : For making combs, electrical switches, handles of utensils and computer discs.

q: <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>Buna</mi><mo>-</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">S</mi></math> can be represented as:
question: <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>Buna</mi><mo>-</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">S</mi></math> can be represented as:
solution: Buna-S is a copolymer of 1,3-butadiene and styrene. It is prepared by copolymerisation of 1,3 butadiene and styrene along with sodium. In this process peroxide is used as a catalyst at 5∘C therefore the formed product is also known as cold rubber.

subject: Chemistry
q: Which among the following is not a polyester ?
question: Which among the following is not a polyester ?
solution: Polyester is any of a group of polymers that consist basically the repeated units of an ester. Polyester is a synthetic fabric that’s usually derived from petroleum. Dacron, PHBV and Glyptal are polyesters. Novolac is a linear condensation polymer of phenol and formaldehyde.

subject: Chemistry
q: Which of the following polymer is a polyamide ?
question: Which of the following polymer is a polyamide ?
solution: Nylon is a polyamide fibre. It is prepared by the condensation polymerisation of adipic acid (HOOC. $\left.\left(\mathrm{CH}_2\right)_4 \mathrm{COOH}\right)$ and hexamethylene diamine $\left(\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{~N} \cdot\left(\mathrm{CH}_2\right)_6 \cdot \mathrm{NH}_2\right)$.

subject: Chemistry
q: Which one of the following is NOT a copolymer?
question: Which one of the following is NOT a copolymer?
solution: A copolymer is a polymer formed when two (or more) different types of monomers are linked in the same polymer chain, as opposed to a homopolymer where only one monomer is used.

The rubber obtained is also called Styrene butadiene rubber (SBR). In Buna-S, Bu stands for butadiene and, Na for sodium and S for styrene. It is vulcanised with sulfur. The rubber is slightly inferior to natural rubber in its physical properties.

Glyptal- a polyester is formed by ethylene glycol and phthalic acid by step-growth polymerization.

Dacron obtained by condensation polymerisation of ethylene glycol and terephthalic acid at 420-460 K using zinc acetate and antimony oxide as catalyst.

q: In polymer Buna-<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">S</mi><mo>:</mo><mo>'</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">S</mi><mo>'</mo></math> stands for:
question: In polymer Buna-<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">S</mi><mo>:</mo><mo>'</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">S</mi><mo>'</mo></math> stands for:
solution:
Buna--S, 'S' stand for styrene.

Buna – S is a more commonly used name for a polymer named styrene butadiene rubber. The structure of Buna – S is given by:

Buna – S is formed using the monomers butadiene and styrene.

Thus in Buna – S, the Bu stands for Butadiene, since butadiene is one of the constituent monomers of the given polymer. Also, Na stands for sodium and S represents the monomer Styrene used for the synthesis of the given polymer.
